description: Fix four bugs in the MCP server's reload, config migration, plugin loader, and self-update paths: (1) sendSyncFull is sent to the extension even when writeAllAdapterFiles fails, causing it to load stale adapters; (2) a failed config migration is retried on every subsequent startup with no backoff or skip; (3) validateTools in loader.ts permits duplicate tool names, causing registry lookup inconsistency; (4) handleServerSelfUpdate always calls process.exit(0) after spawn, even if the new server process failed to start.

userStories.title: Skip sendSyncFull when writeAllAdapterFiles fails during reload
userStories.description: In reload.ts, after `writeAllAdapterFiles(state)` throws, reloadCore catches the error, logs a warning, and continues to `sendSyncFull(state)` unconditionally. The extension then receives a sync that references adapter files that may not have been written, leading to injection failures. Track whether the write succeeded and skip or delay sendSyncFull on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: When `writeAllAdapterFiles` throws, `sendSyncFull` is NOT called immediately after; an appropriate error is logged
When `writeAllAdapterFiles` succeeds, `sendSyncFull` continues to be called as before
The auth-secret-change `sendExtensionReload` path (lines 412–420 in reload.ts) is unaffected

userStories.title: Prevent config migration from retrying permanently when persist fails
userStories.description: In config-migrations.ts, when `atomicWrite` fails (e.g., disk full, wrong permissions), the catch block restores the backup and returns the original (unmigrated) config. On the next startup, the same migration runs again and fails again — an infinite retry loop. Add a sentinel after a failed migration write to prevent repeated attempts in the same session, and log a clear actionable error.
acceptanceCriteria: When `migrateConfig` fails to persist the migrated config, a clear error is logged that includes the config file path and a suggestion to fix the underlying issue (e.g., disk permissions)
The server still starts with the in-memory migrated config (best-effort) even when persist fails
On the next startup (after the issue is resolved), the migration succeeds and is written normally — no permanent state is introduced that would prevent future successful migrations

userStories.title: Detect and reject duplicate tool names in validateTools
userStories.description: In loader.ts, `validateTools` iterates tool definitions and validates individual fields but never checks for duplicate `name` values within the same plugin. Duplicate-named tools cause `toolLookup.set(prefixed, ...)` in registry.ts to silently overwrite earlier entries, and `plugin.tools.find(t => t.name === lookup.toolName)` then returns the first definition while the lookup holds the validator compiled for the last. Detect duplicates in validateTools and return an error.
acceptanceCriteria: `validateTools` returns an `err(...)` result when any two tools in the array share the same `name`
The error message identifies the duplicate name and the plugin source path
Plugins with duplicate tool names are treated as FailedPlugin entries (not loaded)

userStories.title: Fix handleServerSelfUpdate to handle spawn failure before calling process.exit
userStories.description: In extension-handlers.ts, `handleServerSelfUpdate` calls `spawn('opentabs', [...])` and then unconditionally schedules `setTimeout(() => process.exit(0), 200)`. If `spawn` fails (e.g., 'opentabs' is not in PATH because the newly installed version is at a different location), the process exits without a replacement server running. Add an error handler to the child process that cancels the exit and sends an error response to the extension.
acceptanceCriteria: If `child.on('error')` fires before the 200ms exit timer, the timer is cleared and an error response is sent to the extension
If spawn succeeds (no error within 200ms), process.exit(0) fires as before
The error message sent to the extension is descriptive (includes the spawn error message)
